user_message:
Read-only review of v0.3.0 release plan. No file edits. <task> You are a release review engineer doing a second-opinion pass on the v0.3.0 release plan for the EntireContext project. This is READ-ONLY — do not edit any files. Project root: /Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext Plan file: /Users/teslamint/.claude/plans/v0-3-0-resilient-diffie.md Background: - v0.3.0 Close the Loop milestone: 5 epics (E1~E5) merged via PRs #74-78, #80 - Current pyproject.toml version: 0.2.0; v0.2.0 tagged at a1b9519 (2026-04-15) - Local branch feat/e5-quality-loop-main is 4 commits ahead of origin/main but git diff origin/main..HEAD is empty (squash-merged into PR #80) - GitHub milestone v0.3.0: 6/6 closed, CI green - CHANGELOG.md has populated [Unreleased] section (lines 8-20) with all 5 epics - Release workflow fires on tag push v*: lint→test→build→publish(PyPI OIDC)→GitHub Release→close-release-issues - close_release_issues.py parses closing clauses from tagged commit message - test_contract_sync.py::test_schema_version_in_changelog (line 172) requires CHANGELOG paragraph containing both schema and v13 as whole words - v0.3.0 does NOT bump SCHEMA_VERSION (still v13) - Existing v0.2.0 section (line 26) has Database schema v11 → v13 — plan claims this satisfies the test without a new paragraph - Release via PR route (not admin bypass); delete local+remote stale branch after tag Specific questions to investigate: 1. Read tests/test_contract_sync.py lines 160-200 and CHANGELOG.md lines 1-40. Does the test scan ALL of CHANGELOG or only [Unreleased]/latest section? After renaming [Unreleased]→[0.3.0], will the v0.2.0 sections Database schema v11 → v13 still satisfy the regex?
2. Read .github/scripts/close_release_issues.py lines 1-30. Does the regex handle space-only separation like Closes #69 #70 #71 or does it require comma/and? Show the exact regex.
3. Tag placement: After squash-merging release PR to main, the plan says to tag the squash commit on main. Is there an ordering risk where someone might tag before the PR merges, putting the tag on the branch HEAD instead of main?
4. CHANGELOG convention: Does the plan include adding a fresh empty [Unreleased] block above [0.3.0] per Keep a Changelog? Is the edit shape explicit?
5. Check README.md for version badges or version references that need bumping for v0.3.0.
6. Is there a gh command to close the v0.3.0 milestone post-release? Does the plan include this step?
</task>

<grounding_rules>
- Every finding must cite specific file:line
- Do not present inferences as facts; label hypotheses
- Ground claims in actual file content, not assumptions
- Read the actual files before making claims about their content
</grounding_rules>

<structured_output_contract>
Report format: Critical / Important / Suggestion buckets.
Under 400 words total. File:line citations required for each finding.
Focus on things that would actually break the release or produce visible mistakes.
Skip style preferences.
</structured_output_contract>

<default_follow_through_policy>
Read each relevant file before making claims. Do not guess at regex behavior — show the actual regex text. If a file does not exist, say so explicitly.
</default_follow_through_policy>
assistant_summary: 릴리스 플랜의 실제 깨짐 가능성만 보겠습니다. 먼저 관련 메모리/결정 기록을 확인한 뒤, 요청하신 파일들과 플랜 파일을 직접 읽어서 각 질문을 검증하겠습니다.
